sections.title: Cardinal Numbers
sections.explanation: French numbers follow predictable patterns up to 69. From 70, French uses arithmetic: 70 is soixante-dix (60+10), 71 is soixante et onze, 72 is soixante-douze, and so on to 79. 80 is quatre-vingts (4×20) — the -s drops when followed by another number: quatre-vingt-un (81). 90 is quatre-vingt-dix, continuing to 99. 100 is cent; multiples of 100 take -s only when not followed by another number: deux cents (200) but deux cent vingt (220). 1000 is mille and never takes -s. Note: Belgian and Swiss French use septante (70), octante (80), and nonante (90) instead.
sections.tip: Remember the arithmetic logic: soixante-dix = 60+10, quatre-vingts = 4×20. The -s on vingts and cents disappears the moment another number follows. Mille is always invariable.

examples.fr: Il y a soixante-dix élèves dans l'école.
examples.en: There are seventy students in the school.

examples.fr: Nous avons deux cents pages à lire.
examples.en: We have two hundred pages to read.

examples.fr: La ville a deux cent vingt mille habitants.
examples.en: The city has two hundred twenty thousand inhabitants.

examples.fr: C'est la première fois que je viens ici.
examples.en: It is the first time I come here.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 75 in French?
exercises.explanation: In French (France), 75 = soixante-quinze (60+15). Septante-cinq is used in Belgium and Switzerland.

exercises.prompt: How do you write 80 in French?
exercises.explanation: 80 on its own is quatre-vingts with an -s. The -s is dropped only when another number follows (e.g. quatre-vingt-un).
Deux cents takes an -s because it is not followed by another number. If it were deux cent vingt, the -s would drop.

exercises.hint: 90 = quatre-vingt-dix, so 91 = quatre-vingt-onze (no -s on vingt when followed by a number).

exercises.prompt: How do you say 1,000,000 in French?
exercises.explanation: Un million is the correct term. Mille is used for thousands: deux mille = 2,000.

exercises.prompt: Complete: Elle habite au ___ (17) rue Victor Hugo.
What is the ordinal form of 1 (masculine)?
exercises.explanation: The ordinal for 1 is irregular: premier (masculine) / première (feminine). All other ordinals add -ième to the cardinal.

exercises.prompt: Which sentence uses the correct form of cent?
exercises.explanation: Trois cents takes an -s because no number follows. In trois cent vingt, the -s drops because vingt follows.

sections.title: Telling Time
sections.explanation: To ask the time, say Quelle heure est-il? (What time is it?). The answer always starts with Il est. Use Il est une heure for 1:00 (note: une, not un, because heure is feminine). For all other hours use the cardinal number: Il est deux heures, Il est trois heures, etc. To add minutes, simply follow the hour: Il est deux heures dix (2:10). Special expressions: et quart (quarter past), et demie (half past), moins le quart (quarter to). Noon is midi and midnight is minuit — neither uses heure. France commonly uses the 24-hour clock: Il est quatorze heures = 14:00. To specify AM/PM informally: du matin (morning), de l'après-midi (afternoon), du soir (evening).
sections.tip: Watch out for une heure (not un heure) — heure is feminine. Also note: et demie has an -e but midi et demi does not, because midi is masculine.

examples.fr: Quelle heure est-il ? Il est trois heures.
examples.en: What time is it? It is three o'clock.

examples.fr: Il est une heure et quart.
examples.en: It is a quarter past one.

examples.fr: Il est neuf heures et demie.
Il est quatre heures moins le quart.
examples.en: It is a quarter to four.

examples.fr: Le train part à quatorze heures trente.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 'What time is it?' in French?
exercises.explanation: Quelle heure est-il ? is the standard way to ask the time. Quel temps fait-il ? asks about the weather.

exercises.prompt: How do you say 1:00 in French?
exercises.explanation: Heure is feminine, so use une (not un). The singular heure has no -s.

exercises.prompt: Complete: Il est cinq heures ___ (quarter past).
What does 'Il est huit heures moins le quart' mean?
exercises.explanation: Moins le quart means quarter to. Eight o'clock minus a quarter hour = 7:45.

exercises.hint: Midnight has its own special word in French — not minuit heure.

options: Il est deux heures de l'après-midi.
exercises.explanation: Both forms are correct. The 24-hour form (quatorze heures) is common on schedules; the 12-hour form with de l'après-midi is used in conversation.

options: Il est douze heures et demie.
exercises.explanation: Midi is masculine, so demi has no -e. Demie (with -e) is used after heures because heure is feminine.

exercises.prompt: Complete: Le film commence à ___ heures ___ (7:20).
exercises.hint: Just place the minutes after heures with no joining word for plain minutes: sept heures vingt.
